questions.stem: The marketing team _____ the new campaign strategy yesterday.
questions.explanation: The time marker 'yesterday' requires the simple past tense 'approved'.

questions.stem: Ms. Tanaka _____ with the company since 2010.
questions.explanation: 'Since 2010' indicates a period continuing to the present, requiring the present perfect 'has worked'.

questions.stem: The conference _____ place at the Grand Hyatt next month.
questions.explanation: The future time marker 'next month' requires the future tense 'will take'.

questions.stem: By the time the CEO arrives, the team _____ the presentation.
questions.explanation: 'By the time' with a future reference requires the future perfect 'will have finished' to show completion before a future moment.

questions.stem: Each of the applicants _____ to submit a cover letter.
questions.explanation: 'Each' is grammatically singular, so the verb must be 'requires' despite the plural noun 'applicants' that follows.

questions.stem: The report, together with the supporting documents, _____ ready for review.
questions.explanation: The true subject is 'report' (singular); the phrase 'together with the supporting documents' is an interrupter and does not affect the verb.

questions.stem: The _____ of the new policy will take effect next week.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after the article 'the' to serve as the subject of the sentence, so 'implementation' is correct.

questions.stem: The candidate performed _____ during the interview.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the verb 'performed', so 'exceptionally' is correct.

questions.stem: The detailed _____ showed a steady increase in quarterly sales.
questions.explanation: A noun is required as the subject of the verb 'showed'. 'Analysis' is the noun form.

questions.stem: We are looking forward _____ your response at your earliest convenience.
questions.explanation: The phrasal verb 'look forward to' is always followed by 'to'.

questions.stem: The new policy will take effect _____ January 1 of next year.
questions.explanation: 'On' is used with specific dates, while 'in' is used with months or years and 'at' with precise times.

questions.stem: Please refer _____ the attached document for the complete report.
questions.explanation: The verb 'refer' is always followed by the preposition 'to' when directing someone to a source.

questions.stem: The shipment was delayed _____ the bad weather conditions in the region.
questions.explanation: 'Because of' is followed by a noun phrase ('the bad weather conditions'), whereas 'because' requires a full clause.

questions.stem: _____ the budget was limited, the project was completed ahead of schedule.
questions.explanation: 'Although' introduces a contrast between the limited budget and the early completion. 'Despite' would need a noun, not a clause.

questions.stem: The position requires strong communication skills _____ the ability to work under pressure.
questions.explanation: 'As well as' is used to add information, connecting two equally important job requirements without implying a choice.

questions.stem: If the client _____ any changes to the contract, we will review them immediately.
questions.explanation: This is a first conditional (real possibility), which requires the simple present tense in the if-clause and 'will' in the main clause.

questions.stem: We would have finished the project earlier if we _____ more staff available.
questions.explanation: This is a third conditional (past unreal situation), requiring the past perfect 'had had' in the if-clause.

questions.stem: All applicants _____ of the test results by the end of the week.
questions.explanation: Applicants receive the information (passive voice), and 'by the end of the week' indicates future time.

questions.stem: The contract _____ by both parties at the meeting last Friday.
questions.explanation: The contract receives the action of signing (passive), and 'last Friday' indicates past time.

questions.stem: It is recommended that all employees _____ the mandatory training session.
questions.explanation: In the subjunctive structure 'It is recommended that...', the base form of the verb 'attend' is used regardless of the subject.

questions.stem: The new software is _____ than the previous version we were using.
questions.explanation: 'Than' signals a comparative structure, and 'efficient' is a two-syllable adjective that forms the comparative with 'more'.

questions.stem: This is _____ restaurant in the downtown business district.
questions.explanation: The superlative is needed with 'in the downtown business district' (a group of three or more), and 'the' must precede the superlative.

questions.stem: The company plans to _____ its operations into the European market next year.
questions.explanation: 'Expand its operations' is a standard business collocation meaning to increase the scope of business activities.

questions.stem: The two companies signed a _____ to collaborate on the joint research project.
questions.explanation: A 'contract' is a legally binding agreement between parties, commonly used to formalize business collaborations.

questions.stem: We need to _____ a meeting with the stakeholders to discuss the quarterly results.
questions.explanation: 'Schedule a meeting' is a standard business collocation meaning to arrange or plan a meeting at a specific time.

questions.stem: The consultant _____ we hired last month has already improved our workflow.
questions.explanation: 'Whom' is the relative pronoun used as the object of the verb 'hired' and refers to the consultant (a person).

questions.stem: The company _____ headquarters are in Tokyo won the industry award this year.
questions.explanation: 'Whose' is the possessive relative pronoun showing that the headquarters belong to the company.

questions.stem: The manager suggested _____ the deadline to next Friday.
questions.explanation: The verb 'suggest' is followed by a gerund ('extending'), not an infinitive or base form.

questions.stem: We decided _____ the project despite the budget concerns.
questions.explanation: The verb 'decide' is followed by the infinitive 'to continue', not a gerund or base form.

questions.stem: The new manager has _____ excellent understanding of our company procedures.
questions.explanation: 'Understanding' is a singular countable noun here, and 'excellent' begins with a vowel sound, so 'an' is required.

questions.stem: We are looking for _____ experienced accountant to join our finance team.
questions.explanation: 'Accountant' is a singular countable noun introduced for the first time, and 'experienced' begins with a vowel sound, so 'an' is required.

questions.stem: Neither of the candidates _____ suitable for the position.
questions.explanation: 'Neither' is grammatically singular, so the verb must be 'seems' despite the plural noun 'candidates' that follows.

questions.stem: There are _____ applicants than we expected for the job opening.
questions.explanation: 'Fewer' is used with countable nouns like 'applicants'. 'Less' is used with uncountable nouns.

questions.stem: The new employee adapted _____ to the company culture.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the verb 'adapted'. 'Quickly' is the adverb form of 'quick'.

questions.stem: All visitors _____ present a valid ID at the security desk.
questions.explanation: 'Must' expresses a strong requirement or obligation in formal security contexts.

questions.stem: The quarterly report _____ by the end of this week.
questions.explanation: The report receives the action of being completed (passive voice), and 'by the end of this week' indicates future time.

questions.stem: The meeting has been rescheduled _____ Friday to Monday.
questions.explanation: 'Rescheduled from... to...' is the standard pattern used to indicate a change in timing.

questions.stem: The company will expand its operations, _____ the market conditions remain favorable.
questions.explanation: 'Provided that' introduces a condition that must be met for the expansion to happen. It means 'if and only if'.

questions.stem: The _____ of the new software has significantly improved productivity.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after the article 'the' to serve as the subject. 'Installation' is the noun form of 'install'.

questions.stem: The consultant _____ advice we followed saved us thousands of dollars.
questions.explanation: 'Whose' is the possessive relative pronoun showing that the advice belongs to the consultant.

questions.stem: The manager recommended _____ the supplier for better rates.
questions.explanation: The verb 'recommend' is followed by a gerund ('changing'), not an infinitive or base form.

questions.stem: The company is looking for _____ new office in the downtown area.
questions.explanation: 'Office' is a singular countable noun introduced for the first time, and 'new' begins with a consonant sound, so 'a' is required.

questions.stem: The shipment _____ to the wrong address by mistake.
questions.explanation: The shipment receives the action of delivery (passive voice), and the context indicates a past event.

questions.stem: The new model is _____ than the previous version in terms of energy efficiency.
questions.explanation: 'Superior' is a comparative adjective that does not take 'more' or 'er'. It is always followed by 'to', not 'than'.

questions.stem: The company's annual _____ increased by 15% this year.
questions.explanation: 'Revenue' is an uncountable noun in this context, referring to the total income generated by the company.

questions.stem: The collection of antique furniture _____ sold at auction.
questions.explanation: The true subject is 'collection' (singular); the phrase 'of antique furniture' is a prepositional modifier and does not affect the verb.

questions.stem: _____ the client approves the proposal, we will begin work immediately.
questions.explanation: 'As soon as' indicates that the beginning of work will happen immediately after the client's approval.

questions.stem: The training session will focus _____ customer service skills.
questions.explanation: The verb 'focus' is followed by the preposition 'on' when indicating the subject of attention.

questions.stem: The HR department is responsible for employee _____ and training.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after the adjective 'employee' to serve as the object of the preposition 'for'. 'Recruitment' is the noun form.

questions.stem: The project was completed on time, _____ the unexpected challenges.
questions.explanation: 'Despite' is followed by a noun phrase ('the unexpected challenges') and introduces a contrast. 'Although' would need a full clause.

questions.stem: The CEO _____ the company for over two decades before retiring.
questions.explanation: The past perfect 'had led' is used to show an action completed before another past action (retiring).

questions.stem: The marketing team presented _____ findings to the board of directors.
questions.explanation: 'Its' is the possessive pronoun for a singular collective noun like 'team' (when treated as a single unit).

questions.stem: _____ of the employees have completed the mandatory training.
questions.explanation: 'Most' is used with plural countable nouns ('employees') and takes a plural verb ('have'). 'Much' and 'Little' are for uncountable nouns.

questions.stem: The presentation was _____ received by the audience.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the past participle 'received'. 'Well' is the adverb form of 'good'.

questions.stem: You _____ submit the application before the deadline to be considered.
questions.explanation: 'Must' expresses a strong necessity or requirement. The context indicates that submission is mandatory for consideration.

questions.stem: We appreciate your _____ the time to attend this meeting.
questions.explanation: After the possessive pronoun 'your', a gerund ('taking') is required to form a gerund phrase.

questions.stem: The company has established _____ reputation for excellent customer service.
questions.explanation: 'Reputation' is a singular countable noun introduced for the first time, and it begins with a consonant sound, so 'a' is required.

questions.stem: The new policy _____ by the board of directors next month.
questions.explanation: The policy receives the action of approval (passive), and 'next month' indicates future time.

questions.stem: This is _____ efficient manufacturing process we have ever implemented.
questions.explanation: The superlative is needed with 'we have ever implemented' (comparing among all processes), and 'the' must precede the superlative.

questions.stem: The two companies entered into a strategic _____ to develop new technology.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after the article 'a' and the adjective 'strategic'. 'Alliance' is the noun form meaning a formal agreement to cooperate.

questions.stem: The data _____ that consumer confidence is improving.
questions.explanation: 'Data' in formal business English is often treated as a singular uncountable noun, taking the singular verb 'shows'.

questions.stem: If the shipment _____ delayed, we will notify the client immediately.
questions.explanation: This is a first conditional (real possibility), which requires the simple present tense in the if-clause and 'will' in the main clause.

questions.stem: The discount applies _____ orders over $500.
questions.explanation: The verb 'apply' is followed by the preposition 'to' when indicating what something affects or concerns.

questions.stem: The _____ of the merger was announced at the shareholders' meeting.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after the article 'the' to serve as the subject. 'Completion' is the noun form of 'complete'.

questions.stem: The candidate was hired _____ she had the most relevant experience.
questions.explanation: 'Because' introduces a reason clause explaining why the candidate was hired. A full clause follows, so 'because' is correct.

questions.stem: By next March, the company _____ its operations in three new countries.
questions.explanation: 'By next March' with a future reference requires the future perfect 'will have established' to show completion before a future time.

questions.stem: The client expressed _____ satisfaction with the service provided.
questions.explanation: 'Its' is the possessive pronoun for a company or organization treated as a singular entity. 'Client' here refers to a corporate client.

questions.stem: We have received _____ feedback from customers about the new product.
questions.explanation: 'Feedback' is an uncountable noun, so it must be modified by 'a lot of'. 'Many', 'several', and 'a few' are used with countable nouns.

questions.stem: The new system operates _____ than the old one.
questions.explanation: 'Than' signals a comparative structure, and an adverb is needed to modify the verb 'operates'. 'More efficiently' is the correct comparative adverb.

questions.stem: The report _____ be submitted by Friday at the latest.
questions.explanation: 'Must' expresses a strong obligation or requirement. 'By Friday at the latest' indicates a firm deadline.

questions.stem: We look forward to _____ from you soon.
questions.explanation: In the phrasal verb 'look forward to', 'to' is a preposition, so it must be followed by a gerund ('hearing').

questions.stem: The position requires _____ understanding of financial markets.
questions.explanation: 'Understanding' is a singular countable noun here, and it begins with a vowel sound, so 'an' is required.

questions.stem: The documents _____ by the legal department before signing.
questions.explanation: The documents receive the action of review (passive voice), and the context indicates a past event.

questions.stem: The revised proposal is _____ comprehensive than the original.
questions.explanation: 'Than' signals a comparative structure. 'More' is used to form the comparative of the two-syllable adjective 'comprehensive'.

questions.stem: The company decided to _____ its product line to include eco-friendly options.
questions.explanation: After 'to' (infinitive), the base form of the verb is needed. 'Diversify' means to expand the range of products.

questions.stem: The committee _____ divided on the issue of budget allocation.
questions.explanation: When 'committee' refers to the group as a single unit, it takes a singular verb. 'Is divided' treats the committee as one entity.

questions.stem: Had we known about the delay, we _____ an alternative plan.
questions.explanation: This is a third conditional with inversion ('Had we known' = 'If we had known'). The main clause requires 'would have + past participle'.

questions.stem: The success of the project depends _____ the cooperation of all departments.
questions.explanation: The verb 'depend' is always followed by the preposition 'on' when indicating what something relies upon.

questions.stem: The _____ of the new policy was met with mixed reactions.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after the article 'the' to serve as the subject. 'Introduction' is the noun form of 'introduce'.

questions.stem: The company will either reduce costs _____ increase revenue to improve profitability.
questions.explanation: The correlative conjunction pair 'either...or' is used to present two alternatives.

questions.stem: The manager _____ a meeting with the client when I arrived.
questions.explanation: The past continuous 'was having' describes an action in progress when another action ('arrived') interrupted it.

questions.stem: The company values _____ employees and their contributions.
questions.explanation: 'Its' is the possessive pronoun for a company treated as a singular entity, referring to the employees belonging to the company.

questions.stem: _____ information is available on the company's website.
questions.explanation: 'Information' is an uncountable noun, so it must be modified by 'a great deal of'. 'Many', 'several', and 'a few' are for countable nouns.

questions.stem: The new software is _____ compatible with existing systems.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the adjective 'compatible'. 'Fully' is the adverb form of 'full'.

questions.stem: Candidates _____ have at least three years of relevant experience.
questions.explanation: 'Must' expresses a firm requirement in job qualifications. The context indicates this is a mandatory condition.

questions.stem: The company decided _____ its headquarters to a larger facility.
questions.explanation: The verb 'decide' is followed by the infinitive 'to move', not a gerund or base form.

questions.stem: The new employee showed _____ impressive dedication to the project.
questions.explanation: 'Dedication' is an uncountable noun here, but when modified by an adjective ('impressive'), it can take 'an' to indicate a particular type. 'Impressive' begins with a vowel sound.

questions.stem: The results of the survey _____ in next month's newsletter.
questions.explanation: The results receive the action of being published (passive voice), and 'next month' indicates future time.

questions.stem: This is _____ challenging project our team has ever undertaken.
questions.explanation: The superlative is needed with 'we have ever undertaken' (comparing among all projects), and 'the' must precede the superlative.

questions.stem: The company will _____ a new marketing campaign next quarter.
questions.explanation: After the modal 'will', the base form of the verb 'launch' is required. 'Launch a campaign' is a standard business collocation.

questions.stem: The number of employees working remotely _____ increased significantly.
questions.explanation: 'The number of' is always treated as singular, so the verb must be 'has'. 'A number of' would be plural.

passages.title: Email: Office Relocation
passages.text:
Dear Team,

I am writing to inform you that we will be {{0}} to a new office building next quarter. The new space is {{1}} located in the central business district, which will make commuting easier for most employees. All departments {{2}} to complete the move by June 30. Please {{3}} your desk items in advance to ensure a smooth transition.

Best regards,
Sarah Johnson
Operations Manager

items.explanation: 'Will be relocating' is the future continuous tense, used for a planned future action. 'Will be relocated' (passive) would also be grammatically possible but less natural here since 'we' are the ones actively moving.
An adverb is needed to modify the adjective 'located', so 'conveniently' (adverb form of 'convenient') is correct.
The departments are the recipients of the expectation (passive voice), so 'are expected' is correct.
After 'please', the base form of the verb (imperative) is used to give a polite instruction.

passages.title: Memo: New Security Policy
passages.text:
TO: All Staff
FROM: IT Department
DATE: July 15, 2026
SUBJECT: Updated Security Protocols

Please be advised that new security protocols will {{0}} into effect starting next month. All employees {{1}} to change their passwords every 60 days. {{2}}, any unauthorized software must be removed from company devices immediately. Failure to comply {{3}} result in disciplinary action.

Thank you for your cooperation.

items.explanation: After the modal verb 'will', the base form of the verb 'come' is required: 'will come into effect'.
Passive voice is needed because employees are the ones who receive the requirement. 'Are required to change' is the correct form.
A sentence adverb is needed to introduce an additional point. 'Additionally' functions as a connector at the beginning of a sentence.
'Will' expresses certainty of consequence in formal policy language. 'Failure to comply will result in...' is a standard warning formula.

passages.title: Job Advertisement: Marketing Coordinator
passages.text:
Position: Marketing Coordinator

We are looking for a motivated individual to join our {{0}} team. The ideal candidate will have at least two years of {{1}} in digital marketing and social media management. Responsibilities include creating content and {{2}} marketing campaigns across multiple platforms. Applicants must be able to work {{3}} in a fast-paced environment.

To apply, please send your resume and cover letter to careers@example.com.

items.explanation: 'Marketing team' uses 'marketing' as an adjective (a noun functioning attributively) to describe the type of team.
A noun is needed after 'two years of'. 'Experience' is the uncountable noun used for professional background.
Parallel structure with 'creating' requires another gerund. 'Managing' maintains parallel form in the list of responsibilities.
An adverb is needed to modify the verb 'work'. 'Effectively' is the adverb form of 'effective'.

passages.title: Meeting Notice: Quarterly Review
passages.text:
NOTICE: QUARTERLY REVIEW MEETING

Date: August 5, 2026
Time: 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM
Location: Conference Room A

The quarterly review meeting is {{0}} for all department heads. During the meeting, each department will {{1}} its performance for the last quarter and discuss upcoming targets. The CEO has asked that everyone {{2}} the financial reports before attending. If you are unable to attend, please notify {{3}} department manager by August 3.

Thank you.

items.explanation: Passive voice is needed: the meeting is the object of 'require'. 'Is required' indicates obligation in a formal notice.
After the modal 'will', the base form of the verb 'present' is required.
The subjunctive mood is used after 'has asked that', requiring the base form 'review' regardless of the subject.
'Their' is the modern standard singular they possessive pronoun, used here for gender-neutral reference to an unspecified employee.

passages.title: Product Announcement: ConnectPro 3000
passages.text:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

TechCorp is pleased to announce the launch of its latest {{0}}, the ConnectPro 3000. This innovative device {{1}} businesses to manage their network infrastructure more efficiently than ever before. The ConnectPro 3000 will be available for {{2}} starting September 1. Customers who place orders before August 15 will receive a 15% {{3}} on their first purchase.

For more information, please visit www.techcorp.com/connectpro.

items.explanation: A noun is needed after 'its latest'. 'Product' refers to the item being launched. 'Production' would refer to the process, not the item.
The subject 'device' is singular, so the verb must be 'enables' (third person singular present tense).
'Available for purchase' is a standard commercial phrase. 'Purchase' here is a noun following the preposition 'for'.
A noun is needed after 'a 15%'. 'Discount' is the noun form, referring to the price reduction.

passages.title: Email: Project Status Update
passages.text:
From: Mark Robinson <mrobinson@example.com>
To: Project Team
Subject: Q3 Project Status

Dear Team,

I am writing to provide an update on the Q3 projects. The website redesign is currently {{0}} schedule and should be completed by the end of next month. However, the mobile app development has been {{1}} due to some technical challenges. We have {{2}} an additional developer to the team to help address these issues. Please let me know if you have any questions or {{3}} about the current timeline.

Best regards,
Mark

items.explanation: 'On schedule' is a fixed expression meaning proceeding according to the planned timeline.
The present perfect passive 'has been delayed' is needed to indicate that the development was slowed down by technical challenges.
The present perfect 'have assigned' is needed to describe a recent action of adding a developer to the team.
A plural noun is needed to parallel 'questions'. 'Concerns' refers to worries or issues about the timeline.

passages.title: Memo: Budget Reduction
passages.text:
TO: All Department Heads
FROM: Finance Department
DATE: August 1, 2026
SUBJECT: Budget Adjustments for Q4

This memo is to inform you that the company will be {{0}} its budget for the fourth quarter by 10%. This decision was made {{1}} to lower-than-expected revenue in the first half of the year. Each department is required to {{2}} a revised budget proposal by August 15. Please be as {{3}} as possible when identifying areas where costs can be reduced.

Thank you for your cooperation.

items.explanation: The future continuous 'will be reducing' is used for a planned future action. 'Will be reduced' (passive) would also be possible but less natural.
'Due to' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'because of' and is followed by a noun phrase. 'Because' would require a full clause.
After 'required to', the base form of the verb is needed. 'Submit' is the base form following the infinitive marker 'to'.
An adjective is needed after 'as' in the structure 'as + adjective + as possible'. 'Thorough' is the adjective form.

passages.title: Job Advertisement: Senior Software Developer
passages.text:
Position: Senior Software Developer

We are seeking an experienced software developer to join our growing {{0}} team. The successful candidate will be responsible for {{1}} web applications and mentoring junior developers. Applicants should have a bachelor's degree in computer science or a {{2}} field, and at least five years of professional experience. We offer a competitive salary, flexible working hours, and {{3}} health benefits.

To apply, please submit your resume and portfolio to jobs@example.com.

items.explanation: 'Engineering team' uses 'engineering' as an adjective (a noun functioning attributively) to describe the type of team.
After the preposition 'for', a gerund ('developing') is required. 'Responsible for developing' is the correct pattern.
'Related field' uses 'related' as an adjective meaning 'connected or associated with'. 'Computer science or a related field' is a standard phrase.
An adjective is needed to modify 'health benefits'. 'Comprehensive' means complete and covering many aspects.

passages.title: Notice: Holiday Office Closure
passages.text:
NOTICE OF HOLIDAY CLOSURE

Please be advised that the office will be {{0}} from December 24 through January 2 for the holiday season. Regular business hours will {{1}} on January 3. All orders placed during this period will be {{2}} on January 4. We wish you a happy holiday season and a {{3}} New Year!

Thank you for your understanding.

Management

items.explanation: The passive voice 'will be closed' is needed because the office is the object of the closing action.
After the modal 'will', the base form of the verb 'resume' is required. 'Will resume' means will start again.
The future passive 'will be processed' is needed because the orders receive the action of processing.
An adjective is needed to modify 'New Year'. 'Prosperous' is the adjective form meaning successful and thriving.

passages.title: Letter: Customer Appreciation
passages.text:
Dear Valued Customer,

We would like to express our sincere {{0}} for your continued support over the past year. As a token of our appreciation, we are {{1}} you a special discount of 20% on your next purchase. Simply use the code THANKYOU20 when {{2}} your order online. This offer is valid until December 31. We look forward to {{3}} you again soon.

Warm regards,
The Customer Service Team

items.explanation: A noun is needed after the adjective 'sincere'. 'Gratitude' is the noun form meaning thankfulness.
The present continuous 'are offering' is used to describe the current action of providing a discount.
After 'when', a gerund ('placing') is used to form a reduced adverbial clause meaning 'when you are placing your order'.
After 'look forward to', 'to' is a preposition, so it must be followed by a gerund ('serving').

passages.title: Email: Conference Invitation
passages.text:
From: Conference Committee <info@example.com>
To: All Employees
Subject: Annual Industry Conference

Dear Colleagues,

We are pleased to invite you to the Annual Industry Conference, which will be {{0}} on November 15-16 at the City Convention Center. This year's conference will feature {{1}} speakers from leading companies in our sector. Early bird registration is available until October 1, and the {{2}} is $150 per person. Please register {{3}} to secure your spot, as spaces are limited.

We hope to see you there!

items.explanation: The passive voice 'will be held' is needed because the conference is the object of the holding action.

'Will feature' is the active future tense. However, looking at the structure, after 'will feature' we need... Actually, 'will feature' is correct as a verb. Let me reconsider. The blank is after 'will feature' - no, the blank IS 'feature'. The sentence is 'will {{1}} speakers'. So the base form 'feature' is correct after 'will'.
A noun is needed as the subject of the verb 'is'. 'Cost' here means the price or fee for registration.
An adverb is needed to modify the verb 'register'. 'Early' functions as an adverb here, meaning 'before the expected time'.

passages.title: Memo: New Dress Code Policy
passages.text:
TO: All Staff
FROM: Human Resources
DATE: September 10, 2026
SUBJECT: Updated Dress Code

Please be advised that the company dress code will be {{0}} effective October 1. The new policy allows for business casual attire from Monday {{1}} Thursday. On Fridays, employees may wear casual clothing. However, items such as torn jeans and flip-flops are not {{2}}. If you have any questions about the new policy, please {{3} your department manager or contact HR directly.

Thank you for your cooperation.

items.explanation: The passive voice 'will be updated' is needed because the dress code receives the action of being updated.
'From Monday through Thursday' is a standard American English pattern indicating a range of days inclusive.
The passive voice 'are not permitted' is needed because the items are not allowed. 'Permitted' is the past participle.
After 'please', the base form of the verb (imperative) is used to give a polite instruction. 'Consult' means to ask for advice or information.

passages.title: Product Announcement: EcoPack Solution
passages.text:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

GreenPack Inc. is proud to announce the launch of EcoPack, our new {{0}} packaging solution. Made from 100% recycled materials, EcoPack is designed to help businesses {{1}} their environmental impact. The product will be {{2}} in three sizes and is available for order starting next month. Companies that switch to EcoPack will receive a {{3}} on their first bulk order.

For more details, visit www.greenpack.com/ecopack.

items.explanation: An adjective is needed to modify 'packaging solution'. 'Sustainable' means environmentally friendly and able to be maintained over time.
After 'help...', the base form of the verb (infinitive without 'to') is used. 'Help businesses reduce' is the correct pattern.
An adjective is needed after 'will be'. 'Available' means able to be obtained or purchased.
A noun is needed after 'a'. 'Discount' is the noun form referring to a reduction in price.

passages.title: Memo: Employee Training Day
passages.text:
TO: All Employees
FROM: Human Resources
DATE: June 10, 2026
SUBJECT: Annual Employee Training Day

The annual Employee Training Day will be held on Saturday, July 25, 2026, at the Riverside Convention Center, located at 450 Harbor Boulevard. This year's theme is 'Innovation in the Workplace,' and we have arranged a series of workshops covering topics such as time management, digital communication, and leadership development.

All employees are required to attend. The event will begin at 8:30 AM with registration and refreshments, followed by the opening keynote address at 9:00 AM. Workshop sessions will run from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM, with a one-hour lunch break provided. Afternoon sessions will continue from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM.

Transportation will be arranged for employees who do not have personal vehicles. A shuttle bus will depart from the main office parking lot at 7:45 AM sharp. Employees who prefer to drive may park in the convention center's parking garage at no charge.

Please confirm your attendance by June 25 by replying to this email or contacting your department manager. If you have any special dietary requirements for lunch, please indicate them in your response.

passages.title: Email Chain: Client Issue Resolution
passages.text:
Email 1

From: Sarah Chen <schen@example.com>
To: Support Team <support@example.com>
Date: April 3, 2026
Subject: Client Concern - Apex Solutions

Dear Team,

I received a call from Mr. Thompson at Apex Solutions this morning. He expressed frustration that our recent software update caused some of his data to become incorrectly formatted. This issue has significantly delayed their monthly reporting process.

Mr. Thompson mentioned that he contacted our technical support hotline twice but was put on hold for over 15 minutes each time and eventually hung up without speaking to anyone.

Apex Solutions is a major account for us, and I am very concerned about the impact this could have on our business relationship. Please investigate this matter urgently and let me know what steps we can take to resolve the situation.

Best regards,
Sarah

---

Email 2

From: David Kim <dkim@example.com>
To: Sarah Chen <schen@example.com>
Date: April 3, 2026
Subject: Re: Client Concern - Apex Solutions

Hi Sarah,

I apologize for the delay in responding. I have investigated the technical issue and identified the root cause. The update contained a bug that affected data formatting for a small number of users. Our development team has already prepared a fix, and it will be deployed tomorrow morning.

Regarding the support hotline issue, I have reviewed our call logs and confirmed that Mr. Thompson's calls were dropped due to an unusually high call volume that day. I have personally called Mr. Thompson to apologize and explain the situation. He appreciated the call and agreed to give us another chance.

I have also scheduled a follow-up meeting with the Apex Solutions team for next Tuesday to review the fix and ensure everything is working correctly.

Best,
David

items.stem: What is the main issue described in Sarah's email?
options: A client is requesting a refund for unused software
A software update caused data problems for an important client
A support team member was rude to a client on the phone
A client wants to cancel their contract with the company
items.explanation: Sarah reports that the software update 'caused some of his data to become incorrectly formatted,' which is the core technical issue affecting Apex Solutions.

items.stem: What complaint did Mr. Thompson make about the technical support hotline?
options: The hotline was closed when he tried to call
The support staff could not solve his problem
He was kept on hold for an excessive amount of time
He could not find the correct hotline number
items.explanation: Sarah states that Mr. Thompson 'was put on hold for over 15 minutes each time' before he hung up without speaking to anyone.

items.stem: How did David Kim address the technical problem?
options: He asked the client to reinstall the previous version
His team developed a fix that will be released soon
He suggested that the client switch to a different product
He arranged for a partial refund for the inconvenience
items.explanation: David says: 'Our development team has already prepared a fix, and it will be deployed tomorrow morning.'

items.stem: What can be inferred about Mr. Thompson's attitude after David's phone call?
options: He remains dissatisfied with the company
He has decided to seek a different vendor
He is willing to continue working with the company
He is demanding financial compensation for the delay
items.explanation: David reports that Mr. Thompson 'appreciated the call and agreed to give us another chance,' indicating willingness to maintain the business relationship.

items.stem: What is scheduled for next Tuesday?
options: A new software update will be released
A follow-up meeting with the client will take place
The support team will receive additional training
A formal complaint report will be submitted
items.explanation: David states: 'I have also scheduled a follow-up meeting with the Apex Solutions team for next Tuesday to review the fix.'

passages.title: Notice: Parking Lot Renovation
passages.text:
NOTICE OF PARKING LOT CLOSURE

To: All Building Tenants
From: Building Management
Date: September 5, 2026
Subject: Main Parking Lot Resurfacing

Please be advised that the main parking lot will be closed for resurfacing from Monday, September 14, through Friday, September 25, 2026.

During this period, all tenants and visitors are advised to use the temporary parking area located at 1200 Elm Street, approximately two blocks east of the main building. A complimentary shuttle service will operate between the temporary lot and the main entrance every 15 minutes from 6:30 AM to 7:30 PM on weekdays.

Please note that parking permits for the main lot will not be valid during the closure period. Monthly parking pass holders will receive a pro-rated adjustment on their next billing statement to compensate for the inconvenience.

The building management apologizes for any inconvenience this may cause and appreciates your understanding as we make these necessary improvements. For questions or special accommodation requests, please contact the building management office at (555) 0123 or visit Room 101 during regular business hours.

items.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?
options: To announce an increase in parking fees
To inform tenants about temporary parking arrangements
To advertise a new parking facility being built
To remind tenants about building parking regulations
items.explanation: The notice informs tenants about the temporary closure of the main parking lot and provides details about alternative parking and shuttle service.

items.stem: How long will the main parking lot be closed?
items.explanation: The closure runs from Monday, September 14 through Friday, September 25, which is a total of 12 calendar days.

items.stem: How frequently does the shuttle service operate?
items.explanation: The notice states the shuttle operates 'every 15 minutes from 6:30 AM to 7:30 PM on weekdays.'

items.stem: The word 'complimentary' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
items.explanation: In this context, 'complimentary' means provided free of charge, at no cost to the users of the shuttle service.

items.stem: What will happen to monthly parking pass holders?
options: They will receive a full refund for the closure period
They will be charged an additional fee for alternative parking
They will get a proportional adjustment on their next bill
They will need to purchase a temporary parking permit
items.explanation: The notice states: 'Monthly parking pass holders will receive a pro-rated adjustment on their next billing statement,' meaning a proportional credit.

passages.title: Advertisement: Office Space for Rent
passages.text:
Prime Office Space Available in Downtown District

A prestigious office space on the 15th floor of the City Tower building is now available for lease. Located at the intersection of Main Street and Park Avenue, this location offers unparalleled access to public transportation, restaurants, and banking facilities.

The space measures approximately 2,500 square feet and includes three private offices, an open-plan work area, a conference room that seats up to 12 people, and a kitchenette. Large windows provide natural lighting throughout the day.

Rent is $4,500 per month, which includes utilities, cleaning services, and 24-hour building security. A three-year lease is required, with an option to renew. The space is available for immediate occupancy.

For a private tour, please contact Linda Martinez at (555) 0456 or visit our leasing office in Room 200 of the City Tower building, Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

passages.title: Notice: Fire Drill Procedure
passages.text:
FIRE DRILL NOTICE

To: All Building Occupants
From: Building Safety Office
Date: November 1, 2026
Subject: Quarterly Fire Drill

A quarterly fire drill will be conducted on Friday, November 13, 2026, at 10:00 AM. The drill is expected to last approximately 15 minutes.

When the fire alarm sounds, please follow these procedures:

1. Immediately stop what you are doing and turn off any equipment that could be a fire hazard.
2. Close all windows and doors behind you as you leave.
3. Proceed calmly to the nearest emergency exit. Do not use elevators.
4. Gather at the designated assembly point in the north parking lot.
5. Wait for further instructions from the floor warden or safety personnel.

Please note that the fire alarm will be tested at 9:45 AM with a short ring to confirm the system is working. The continuous alarm at 10:00 AM signals the start of the actual drill.

Employees with mobility impairments should proceed to the designated refuge areas on each floor and wait for assistance. Floor wardens have been trained to assist with evacuation.

Failure to participate in the drill may result in a written warning. Your cooperation is essential to ensure everyone's safety.

items.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?
options: To report a recent fire incident in the building
To inform occupants about an upcoming safety drill
To announce new fire safety equipment installation
To request volunteers for the safety committee
items.explanation: The notice informs building occupants about the quarterly fire drill, including the date, time, and procedures to follow.

items.stem: What should occupants do when the fire alarm sounds?
options: Wait for a floor warden to guide them
Gather in the main lobby of the building
Proceed calmly to the nearest emergency exit
Call the building safety office for instructions
items.explanation: The notice instructs occupants to 'Proceed calmly to the nearest emergency exit' and not to use elevators.

items.stem: What happens at 9:45 AM on the day of the drill?
options: The fire alarm is briefly tested
A safety meeting is held in the lobby
items.explanation: The notice states: 'the fire alarm will be tested at 9:45 AM with a short ring to confirm the system is working.'

items.stem: What is the consequence of not participating in the drill?
options: A written warning may be issued
The employee will be sent home
items.explanation: The notice states: 'Failure to participate in the drill may result in a written warning.'

items.stem: Where is the designated assembly point?
items.explanation: The notice instructs occupants to 'Gather at the designated assembly point in the north parking lot.'

passages.title: Article: Industry Trends Report
passages.text:
The Future of Retail: Key Trends for 2026

By Emily Watson, Industry Analyst

The retail industry is undergoing a significant transformation driven by changing consumer behaviors and technological advancements. According to a recent report by Market Research Group, three major trends are shaping the sector this year.

First, omnichannel shopping has become the standard rather than the exception. Consumers now expect to seamlessly switch between online browsing and in-store purchasing. Retailers that have integrated their online and offline channels report a 35% increase in customer retention.

Second, artificial intelligence is revolutionizing inventory management. AI-powered systems can predict demand with up to 95% accuracy, reducing overstock by 40% and preventing stockouts. Major retailers like ShopMart and ValuePlus have already implemented these systems.

Third, sustainability is no longer optional. A survey conducted in January found that 72% of consumers prefer to buy from brands with transparent environmental practices. Retailers are responding by adopting eco-friendly packaging and reducing their carbon footprint.

However, challenges remain. Supply chain disruptions continue to affect delivery times, and rising operational costs are putting pressure on profit margins. Industry experts recommend that businesses invest in automation and data analytics to stay competitive.

items.stem: What is the main topic of this article?
options: The history of the retail industry
Current trends shaping the retail sector
A comparison of different retail companies
The impact of online shopping on small businesses
items.explanation: The article discusses three major trends currently shaping the retail industry: omnichannel shopping, AI in inventory management, and sustainability.

items.stem: According to the article, what benefit have retailers seen from integrating online and offline channels?
options: A 50% growth in new customer acquisition
items.explanation: The article states: 'Retailers that have integrated their online and offline channels report a 35% increase in customer retention.'

items.stem: What percentage of consumers prefer sustainable brands according to the survey mentioned?
items.explanation: The article states: '72% of consumers prefer to buy from brands with transparent environmental practices.'

items.stem: The word 'seamlessly' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
items.explanation: 'Seamlessly' means smoothly and without interruption, like a seamless transition between different shopping channels.

items.stem: What challenge does the article mention retailers are currently facing?
items.explanation: The article mentions: 'Supply chain disruptions continue to affect delivery times, and rising operational costs are putting pressure on profit margins.'

passages.title: Email Chain: Vendor Selection
passages.text:
Email 1

From: Robert Chen <rchen@example.com>
To: Procurement Team
Date: June 5, 2026
Subject: Vendor Proposals for Office Supplies

Dear Team,

We have received proposals from three vendors for our office supply contract. After reviewing the submissions, I recommend that we consider Vendor A and Vendor C for further evaluation.

Vendor A offers the most competitive pricing, with a 15% discount on bulk orders. However, their delivery times are slightly longer than the industry average. Vendor C has the fastest delivery times and offers a wider product range, but their prices are 10% higher than Vendor A's.

Vendor B's proposal was incomplete and did not meet several of our requirements, so I suggest we eliminate them from consideration.

Please review the attached comparison sheet and share your thoughts by Friday.

Best,
Robert

---

Email 2

From: Lisa Park <lpark@example.com>
To: Robert Chen <rchen@example.com>
Date: June 6, 2026
Subject: Re: Vendor Proposals for Office Supplies

Hi Robert,

I have reviewed the comparison sheet. I agree that Vendor B should be eliminated. Between Vendor A and Vendor C, I think we should go with Vendor A. While their delivery times are not the fastest, the cost savings are significant, and we can place orders earlier to account for the longer delivery window.

However, I noticed that Vendor C offers eco-friendly product options, which aligns with our company's sustainability goals. Could we ask Vendor A if they can match some of Vendor C's eco-friendly offerings?

Let's discuss this in our meeting next Tuesday.

Best,
Lisa

items.stem: What is the main purpose of Robert's email?
options: To announce a new office supply contract
To recommend vendors for further evaluation
To complain about a vendor's incomplete proposal
To request approval for a budget increase
items.explanation: Robert recommends considering Vendor A and Vendor C for further evaluation and shares his analysis of each vendor's strengths and weaknesses.

items.stem: What is mentioned as a disadvantage of Vendor A?
options: Their delivery times are longer than average
items.explanation: Robert states: 'their delivery times are slightly longer than the industry average' as a disadvantage of Vendor A.

items.stem: Why was Vendor B eliminated from consideration?
options: They did not respond to the request
items.explanation: Robert states: 'Vendor B's proposal was incomplete and did not meet several of our requirements, so I suggest we eliminate them.'

items.stem: What additional concern does Lisa raise about Vendor A?
options: They have a minimum order requirement
items.explanation: Lisa notes that Vendor C offers eco-friendly options and suggests asking Vendor A if they can match some of those offerings.

items.stem: What does Lisa suggest doing next?
options: Asking Vendor A about eco-friendly options
Requesting a new proposal from Vendor B
items.explanation: Lisa suggests: 'Could we ask Vendor A if they can match some of Vendor C's eco-friendly offerings?'

passages.title: Notice: Building Maintenance Schedule
passages.text:
BUILDING MAINTENANCE NOTICE

To: All Tenants
From: Building Management
Date: February 1, 2026
Subject: Scheduled Maintenance for February

Please be advised of the following scheduled maintenance activities for the month of February:

Elevator Maintenance
Date: February 8-9 (Saturday and Sunday)
Time: 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM each day
Details: Both elevators will undergo routine inspection and servicing. During this time, elevators will be available one at a time. Some delays are expected, so please allow extra time for travel between floors.

HVAC System Upgrade
Date: February 15-16 (Saturday and Sunday)
Time: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM each day
Details: The heating, ventilation, and air conditioning system will be upgraded on the 5th and 6th floors. These floors will experience temporary temperature fluctuations. All other floors will not be affected.

Fire Safety Inspection
Date: February 22 (Saturday)
Time: 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM
Details: The annual fire safety inspection will be conducted. Fire alarms may be tested intermittently during this period. No action is required from tenants.

We apologize for any inconvenience these activities may cause. For urgent maintenance issues, please contact the building management office at (555) 0111.

items.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?
options: To announce a rent increase for the coming month
To inform tenants about upcoming maintenance work
To request that tenants report maintenance issues
To introduce a new building management team
items.explanation: The notice informs tenants about three scheduled maintenance activities for February: elevator maintenance, HVAC upgrade, and fire safety inspection.

items.stem: What should tenants expect during the elevator maintenance?
options: Both elevators will be completely out of service
Only one elevator will operate at a time
Elevators will run on a reduced schedule
The elevators will be replaced with new ones
items.explanation: The notice states: 'During this time, elevators will be available one at a time. Some delays are expected.'

items.stem: Which floors will be affected by the HVAC system upgrade?
items.explanation: The notice states: 'The heating, ventilation, and air conditioning system will be upgraded on the 5th and 6th floors.'

items.stem: What is the duration of the fire safety inspection?
items.explanation: The notice states the fire safety inspection will take place from '9:00 AM - 12:00 PM,' which is three hours.

items.stem: The word 'intermittently' in the fire safety section is closest in meaning to:
items.explanation: 'Intermittently' means occurring at irregular intervals, not continuously. The fire alarms may sound occasionally during the inspection.
